subject: ap-psychology
title: Research Methods & Experimental Design
stimulus:
Dr. Knowles is interested in exploring the relationship between garment cost and perceptions of clothing
quality. She predicts that higher-priced clothing will be perceived as being of higher quality. She creates a W eb
site that presents floral shirts listed at a variety of prices. Participants are directed to the W eb site, where they
are asked to rate the quality of each shirt (on a 1 to 10 scale, with higher numbers meaning better quality). T he
data collected are presented in the scatterplot.

parts.prompt: State the hypothesis that Dr. Knowles tested in the study.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that Dr. Knowles hypothesized that higher priced clothing would be perceived as being of higher quality. OR The response must indicate that there would be a positive/direct correlation (e.g., relationship, association, etc.) between price and perceived quality.
Acceptable: The perceived quality of the shirts is positively correlated with price.

parts.prompt: Identify the operational definition Dr. Knowles used for quality.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that Dr. Knowles operationally defined quality as score on the rating scale.
Acceptable: Dr. Knowles operationally defined quality as a score from 1 to 10.

parts.prompt: Identify the type of relationship found between the variables of interest in the study.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that the relationship between the variables is a negative/inverse correlation/relationship/association.
Acceptable: It is a negative correlation.

parts.prompt: Explain why Dr. Knowles cannot generalize the results of her study to the general population.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: population. The response must indicate that results cannot be generalized because of small sample size. OR The response must indicate the sample is not representative of the general population.
Acceptable: Dr. Knowles can’t generalize because there are only 10 subjects.; This study’s results cannot be generalized to the population because it is likely the

parts.prompt: site. Explain how Mere-exposure effect relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that repeated experience with the shirts or the Web site leads to increased liking of the shirts.
Acceptable: The more often that people look at the Web site the more they like the shirts.

parts.prompt: site. Explain how Cones of the retina relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that cones enable the perception of color or fine detail of the floral shirts.
Acceptable: Cones help people see the color of the shirts.; Malfunction of cones hinders people’s ability to see the colors of the shirts.

parts.prompt: site. Explain how Prototype relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that the participants’ perceptions of the shirts are influenced by their idea of the best, ideal, or most typical shirt.
Acceptable: Participants compare their ideal of what a floral shirt should look like to the shirts on
